title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score, 2019

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

This choropleth map displays the Physical Integrity Rights Score for 2019, a metric ranging from -4 to 4 where large positive scores indicate that government abuses such as killings, torture, and political imprisonment are rare, while large negative scores signify that such abuses are relatively widespread. The visualization uses a continuous color scale transitioning from dark red for the lowest scores to dark blue for the highest, with white representing a neutral score of zero. The highest values, falling in the 3 to 4 band, are visible across western and central Europe, including France, Germany, and the Nordic countries, as well as in Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. In the Americas, the United States and Argentina also appear in the upper positive bands. Conversely, the lowest scores, situated in the -3 to -4 range, are concentrated in specific regions of the Middle East and North Africa, with countries like Algeria, Yemen, and Sudan shaded in the darkest red tones. India and parts of Southeast Asia occupy the lower negative bands between -1 and -2. Large portions of sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South America show scores hovering near the zero mark or in the slightly negative range. Greenland and several nations in central Africa are marked with diagonal hatching, indicating that no data is available for these territories. The scores are based on a statistical model that combines measures from several other sources. The global distribution shows a distinct concentration of high protection scores in the Global North and Oceania, while the most severe deficits in physical integrity rights are clustered in the Middle East and North Africa.
